goal: review cxs recent-query CLI design and update skill guidance

summary.title: Add coverage-first query checks and recent keyword sorting to cxs
summary.what: Added a read-only status --selector coverage check with requestedCoverage/recommendedAction, extended find with explicit relevance|ended|started sorting and repeatable --exclude-session, documented the coverage-first workflow across README, AGENTS, coverage design docs, and both cxs/cxsd skill packages, and bumped the package to 0.3.1.
summary.why: Agents were confusing sync with querying and treating default relevance-ranked find results as newest sessions. The change makes freshness checks explicit without adding a new top-level recent command or allowing read commands to write the index.
summary.user_goal: Review whether the CLI command design fits the project principles, then update the CLI and skills so latest keyword queries and selector coverage checks are hard to misuse, and prepare the result for release.

decisions.chose: Add status --selector as the read-only way to inspect requested selector freshness and recommendedAction.
decisions.rationale: This keeps sync as the only writer while giving agents a direct way to decide whether a query can run without another sync.
rejected: Using stats.sessionCount as the freshness contract

decisions.chose: Extend find with --sort relevance|ended|started and repeatable --exclude-session.
decisions.rationale: The existing find command already owns keyword recall; explicit time sorting and self-hit exclusion solve the latest-session bug without adding a new top-level command.

decisions.chose: Update both cxs and cxsd skills and references with coverage-first guidance while keeping cxsd examples on CXSD_BIN/cxsd.
decisions.rationale: Published and dev skills should describe the same product semantics, but cxsd must remain a local checkout wrapper.

rejected.alternative: Add a new cxs recent top-level command
rejected.reason: It would add a separate concept when explicit find sorting is sufficient.

rejected.alternative: Run sync before every query in skill guidance
rejected.reason: sync is only needed when selector coverage is missing or stale.

rejected.alternative: Let read commands implicitly refresh indexes
rejected.reason: This violates the repository invariant that sync is the only write command.

risks: find --sort ended still recalls a bounded candidate set before final time sorting; extremely common keywords could miss an older relevant result outside the recall window, though latest-session queries should bias toward recent rows and tests cover the intended self-hit path.
status --selector computes source snapshots for the requested selector, so very broad all(root) checks scan raw metadata and can be slower on very large session trees.

anti_patterns.what: Call sync unconditionally before every query
anti_patterns.why: status --selector now exposes whether coverage is fresh; unnecessary sync wastes time and can rewrite index state without need.

anti_patterns.what: Treat default find order as chronological
anti_patterns.why: Default find order is relevance; latest/recent questions require --sort ended or list --sort ended.

anti_patterns.what: Make find/list/read commands write the SQLite index or scan raw message content
anti_patterns.why: The project boundary keeps sync as the only writer and content answers sourced from the index.

followups: Publish @act0r/cxs@0.3.1 and reinstall the global published cxs skill after the repo changes are pushed.
If latest keyword queries need stronger recall for very broad terms, consider a future SQL path that intersects recent session rows with indexed matches rather than increasing the recall window.

architectural_claims: status remains a planning/read-only command and sync remains the only index writer.
Selector coverage and freshness are first-class CLI outputs for agents via requestedCoverage.
Recent keyword lookup is represented by explicit sorting on find instead of a separate command.
behavioral_changes: cxs status accepts --selector and returns requestedCoverage with complete, freshness, source snapshot counts, covering selectors, and recommendedAction.
cxs find accepts --sort relevance|ended|started and repeatable --exclude-session.
find JSON includes sort and excludedSessions metadata.
Human status output prints requested coverage freshness and recommended action when --selector is used.
security_implications: No new credential handling or external network behavior was added.
status --selector reads raw session metadata only; content answers still come from the SQLite index.
migration_notes: Existing indexes remain compatible; users should rely on status --selector to decide whether to sync selector coverage.
